title: The Tokenizer
author: Zachary Proser
description: How tokenization works: BPE tokenizer training, ordered merges, byte fallback, token IDs, costs, multilingual tradeoffs, and why LLMs use tokens.

blueprint.subject: TOKENIZATION
blueprint.eyebrow: Text into model inputs · Complete working drawing
blueprint.subtitle: A tokenizer decides which pieces of language the model can see, how many positions they consume, and what every request costs.
rfiSuggestions: Why does the same text use different token counts across models?
How does BPE learn and replay an ordered merge table?
When should a model use subwords, bytes, or no fixed tokenizer?
